name: Basketball
weather_note: Early June peak pre-monsoon heat in Marredpally. Low rain chance (~10%). Humidity around 45%. Schedule matches early morning and stay hydrated.
rules_text:
1. Team Composition
Each team shall consist of 5 players on court.
A minimum of 1 female player must be present on the court at all times.
Each team may register up to 3 substitute players, including at least 1 female substitute.


2. Match Format
Each match shall be played for 10 minutes.( 5 minute halves with 5 minute break time at half time )
The team with the higher score at the end of regulation time shall be declared the winner.
Standard basketball rules shall apply unless otherwise specified by the tournament organizers.



3. Tournament Format
The tournament shall follow a Round Robin Format.
Teams shall be assigned as Team A, Team B, and Team C through a draw of QR codes.
Matches shall be conducted in the following order:
Team A vs Team B
Team B vs Team C
Team C vs Team A
The team losing Match 1 shall play Match 2 against Team C.
If the losing team from Match 1 also loses Match 2, the Final shall be conducted between the remaining two teams.
If each team records one win and one loss, the total points scored across all league matches shall be considered and the top two teams shall qualify for the Final.
If two teams remain tied on total points scored, a 5-point Tie-Breaker Match shall be conducted to determine qualification for the Final.

4. Final Match
The two qualified teams shall compete in the Final.
The team with the higher score at the end of regulation time shall be declared the Champion.

5. Tie-Breaker Rules
If the Final ends in a tie, the total points scored across all tournament matches (including league matches) shall be considered.
The team with the higher cumulative tournament score shall be declared the Winner.
If the cumulative tournament scores are also equal, a 5-point Tie-Breaker Match shall be played.
The winner of the Tie-Breaker Match shall be declared the Champion.

6. Referees & Officials
All matches shall be conducted under the supervision of appointed referees and tournament officials.
Standard basketball rules shall govern gameplay, substitutions, fouls, and scoring.
Any dispute arising during the tournament shall be resolved by the referees and tournament officials.
The decision of the referees and tournament officials shall be final and binding on all participants.

7. Awards
 Winners: 300 Team Points
 Runners-Up: 150 Team Points

name: Throwball
weather_note: Mid-June monsoon onset. Light showers likely — have backup indoor option ready.
rules_text:
1. Team Composition
Each team shall consist of 7 players on court.
A minimum of 2 female players must be present on the court at all times.
Each team may register up to 3 substitute players.
2. Match Format
Each match shall be played as a Best of 3 Sets format.
Each set shall be played up to 11 points.
The team winning 2 sets shall be declared the winner of the match.
Standard Throwball rules shall apply unless otherwise specified by the tournament organizers.
3. Tournament Format
The tournament shall follow a Round Robin Format.
Teams shall be assigned as Team A, Team B, and Team C through a draw of QR codes.
Matches shall be conducted in the following order:
Team A vs Team B
Team B vs Team C
Team C vs Team A
The team losing Match 1 shall play Match 2 against Team C.
If the losing team from Match 1 also loses Match 2, the Final shall be conducted between the remaining two teams.
If each team records one win and one loss, the total points scored across all league matches shall be considered and the top two teams shall qualify for the Final.
If two teams remain tied on total points scored, a 5-point Tie-Breaker Match shall be conducted to determine qualification for the Final.
4. Final Match
The two qualified teams shall compete in the Final.
The Final shall be played as a Best of 3 Sets format.
Each set shall be played up to 11 points.
The team winning 2 sets shall be declared the Champion.
5. Tie-Breaker Rules
If the Final ends in a tie, the total points scored across all tournament matches (including league matches) shall be considered.
The team with the higher cumulative tournament score shall be declared the Winner.
If the cumulative tournament scores are also equal, a 5-point Tie-Breaker Match shall be played.
The winner of the Tie-Breaker Match shall be declared the Champion.
6. Referees & Officials
All matches shall be conducted under the supervision of appointed referees and tournament officials.
Standard Throwball rules shall govern gameplay, service, catches, throws, faults, substitutions, and scoring.
Any dispute arising during the tournament shall be resolved by the referees and tournament officials.
The decision of the referees and tournament officials shall be final and binding on all participants.
7. Awards
Winners: 300 Team Points
Runners-Up: 150 Team Points

name: Tug of War
weather_note: Ground may be slightly muddy — check terrain before matches.
rules_text:
1. Team Composition
Each team shall consist of 10 players on the rope.
A minimum of 3 female players must be on the rope at all times.
Each team may register up to 5 substitute players.
Substitutions may only be made between matches and not during an ongoing match, except in case of injury and with referee approval.
2. Match Format
Each match shall be played on a Best of 3 Sets basis.
The first team to win 2 sets shall be declared the winner of the match.
A set shall be won when a team successfully pulls the opposing team's side marker across the designated winning line as determined by the referee.
A center mark shall be placed on the rope, along with two side markers positioned at equal distances from the center mark.
A center line shall be marked on the ground using paint, tape, or other suitable markings.
The referee shall use these markings to determine the winner of each pull in accordance with standard Tug of War rules.
3. Tournament Format
The tournament shall follow a Round Robin Format.
Teams shall be assigned as Team A, Team B, and Team C through a draw of QR codes.
Matches shall be conducted in the following order:
Team A vs Team B
Team B vs Team C
Team C vs Team A
The team losing Match 1 shall compete in Match 2 against Team C.
If the losing team from Match 1 also loses Match 2, the Final shall be conducted between the remaining two teams.
If each team records one win and one loss, the best overall winning pull timings from the league stage shall be considered and the top two teams shall qualify for the Final.
If two teams remain tied on timings, a Tie-Breaker Pull shall be conducted to determine qualification for the Final.
4. Final Match
The two qualified teams shall compete in the Final.
The Final shall be played on a Best of 3 Sets basis.
The team winning 2 sets shall be declared the Champion.
5. Tie-Breaker Rules
If qualification for the Final results in a tie, the best overall winning pull timings from the league stage shall be considered.
The team with the better timing shall be ranked higher.
If the timings are also equal, a Tie-Breaker Pull shall be conducted.
If the Final ends in a tie, a decisive Tie-Breaker Pull shall be conducted.
The winner of the Tie-Breaker Pull shall be declared the Champion.
6. Referees & Officials
All matches shall be conducted under the supervision of appointed referees and tournament officials.
Standard Tug of War rules shall govern rope handling, pulling techniques, fouls, and match procedures.
Any dispute arising during the tournament shall be resolved by the referees and tournament officials.
The decision of the referees and tournament officials shall be final and binding on all participants.
7. Awards
Winners: 300 Team Points
Runners-Up: 150 Team Points
Gifts and Certificates will be distributed on the same day.

name: Lemon & Spoon Race
weather_note: Quick fun race format with heats and finals.
rules_text:
General Team Registration
Each team may register up to 2 substitute participants for the event.
Substitutes may be utilized across the Male, Female, and Relay Categories before the commencement of the respective event.
Once a participant has begun competing in a category, substitutions shall not be permitted except with referee approval in case of injury.

MALE CATEGORY
1. Team Composition
Each team may nominate 2 male participants.
A total of 6 participants shall compete in this category (2 participants from each of the 3 teams).
2. Match Format
All participants shall compete individually.
The objective is to complete the designated course in the shortest possible time while balancing the lemon on the spoon.
Timing shall begin when the referee signals the start.
Timing shall end when the participant crosses the finish line.
3. Tournament Format
The preliminary stage shall consist of 2 sets.
Each set shall have 3 participants, with 1 participant from each team competing simultaneously.
At the end of both sets, the 3 fastest valid participants shall qualify for the Final.
4. Final Match & Tie-Breaker Rules
The 3 qualified participants shall compete simultaneously in the Final.
The participant recording the fastest valid time in the Final shall be declared the Winner.
The participant recording the second-fastest valid time in the Final shall be declared the Runner-Up.
If two or more participants are tied for qualification to the Final, a Tie-Breaker Race shall be conducted.
If the Final results in a tie, a Tie-Breaker Race shall be conducted immediately.
The participant recording the faster valid time in the tie-breaker shall be ranked higher.
5. Penalties & Disqualifications
Penalty Offences
If the lemon falls from the spoon at any point during the race, the participant must immediately return to the original starting line and restart the race from the beginning.
The timer shall continue running during the restart.
There is no limit on the number of restarts.
Disqualification Offences
Deliberately holding, securing, or balancing the lemon with any part of the body other than the spoon.
Deliberately obstructing or interfering with another participant.
Leaving the designated lane.
Starting before the referee's signal.
Receiving physical assistance from another person.
Unsportsmanlike conduct as determined by the referee.
6. Referees & Officials
The event shall be conducted under the supervision of appointed referees and officials.
Any dispute arising during the event shall be resolved by the referees and officials.
The decision of the referees and event officials shall be final and binding on all participants.
7. Awards
Winner: 100 Team Points
Runner-Up: 50 Team Points

FEMALE CATEGORY
1. Team Composition
Each team may nominate 2 female participants.
A total of 6 participants shall compete in this category (2 participants from each of the 3 teams).
2. Match Format
All participants shall compete individually.
The objective is to complete the designated course in the shortest possible time while balancing the lemon on the spoon.
Timing shall begin when the referee signals the start.
Timing shall end when the participant crosses the finish line.
3. Tournament Format
The preliminary stage shall consist of 2 sets.
Each set shall have 3 participants, with 1 participant from each team competing simultaneously.
At the end of both sets, the 3 fastest valid participants shall qualify for the Final.
4. Final Match & Tie-Breaker Rules
The 3 qualified participants shall compete simultaneously in the Final.
The participant recording the fastest valid time in the Final shall be declared the Winner.
The participant recording the second-fastest valid time in the Final shall be declared the Runner-Up.
If two or more participants are tied for qualification to the Final, a Tie-Breaker Race shall be conducted.
If the Final results in a tie, a Tie-Breaker Race shall be conducted immediately.
The participant recording the faster valid time in the tie-breaker shall be ranked higher.
5. Penalties & Disqualifications
Penalty Offences
If the lemon falls from the spoon at any point during the race, the participant must immediately return to the original starting line and restart the race from the beginning.
The timer shall continue running during the restart.
There is no limit on the number of restarts.
Disqualification Offences
Deliberately holding, securing, or balancing the lemon with any part of the body other than the spoon.
Deliberately obstructing or interfering with another participant.
Leaving the designated lane.
Starting before the referee's signal.
Receiving physical assistance from another person.
Unsportsmanlike conduct as determined by the referee.
6. Referees & Officials
The event shall be conducted under the supervision of appointed referees and officials.
Any dispute arising during the event shall be resolved by the referees and officials.
The decision of the referees and event officials shall be final and binding on all participants.
7. Awards
Winner: 100 Team Points
Runner-Up: 50 Team Points

RELAY CATEGORY
1. Team Composition
Each team shall consist of 4 participants.
Teams may decide their own running order before the race begins.
Registered substitutes may be used prior to the start of the race in accordance with event substitution rules.
2. Match Format
Each participant must complete their section of the course while balancing the lemon on the spoon.
The spoon and lemon must be handed over to the next participant within the designated exchange zone.
3. Tournament Format
All three teams shall compete simultaneously in a single relay race.
The team completing the relay in the shortest valid time shall be declared the Winner.
The team with the second-shortest valid time shall be declared the Runner-Up.
4. Final Match & Tie-Breaker Rules
No separate Final shall be conducted.
In the event of a tie, a Tie-Breaker Relay Race shall be conducted.
5. Penalties & Disqualifications
Penalty Offences
If the lemon falls during any leg of the relay, the participant must return to the starting point of their leg and restart that leg.
The timer shall continue running during the restart.
Disqualification Offences
Deliberately holding, securing, or balancing the lemon with any part of the body other than the spoon.
Improper exchange outside the designated exchange zone.
Deliberately obstructing another team.
Leaving the designated lane.
Starting before the referee's signal.
Receiving physical assistance from another person.
Unsportsmanlike conduct as determined by the referee.
6. Referees & Officials
The event shall be conducted under the supervision of appointed referees and officials.
Any dispute arising during the event shall be resolved by the referees and officials.
The decision of the referees and event officials shall be final and binding on all participants.
7. Awards
Winner: 100 Team Points
Runner-Up: 50 Team Points

name: Sack Race
weather_note: Track should be dry and safe for participants.
rules_text:
General Team Registration
Each team may register up to 3 substitute participants for the event.
At least 1 substitute must be female.
Substitutes may be utilized across the Male, Female, and Relay Categories before the commencement of the respective event.
Once a participant has begun competing in a category, substitutions shall not be permitted except with referee approval in case of injury.

MALE CATEGORY
1. Team Composition
Each team may nominate 2 male participants.
A total of 6 participants shall compete in this category (2 participants from each of the 3 teams).
2. Match Format
All participants shall compete individually.
The objective is to complete the designated course in the shortest possible time while remaining inside the sack at all times.
Timing shall begin when the referee signals the start.
Timing shall end when the participant crosses the finish line.
3. Tournament Format
The preliminary stage shall consist of 2 sets.
Each set shall have 3 participants, with 1 participant from each team competing simultaneously.
At the end of both sets, the 3 fastest valid participants shall qualify for the Final.
4. Final Match & Tie-Breaker Rules
The 3 qualified participants shall compete simultaneously in the Final.
The participant recording the fastest valid time in the Final shall be declared the Winner.
The participant recording the second-fastest valid time in the Final shall be declared the Runner-Up.
If two or more participants are tied for qualification to the Final, a Tie-Breaker Race shall be conducted.
If the Final results in a tie, a Tie-Breaker Race shall be conducted immediately.
5. Penalties & Disqualifications
Penalty Offences
If a participant falls, they must get up and continue from the point at which they fell.
No restart or re-run shall be granted for falls.
The sack must remain above the knees throughout the race.
If the sack falls below the knees and the referee determines that an advantage has been gained, the participant must return to their original starting position and restart the race.
The timer shall continue running during the restart.
Disqualification Offences
Intentionally pushing, shoving, or obstructing another participant.
Leaving the designated lane.
Receiving physical assistance from any person.
Running outside the sack.
Deliberately removing the sack before crossing the finish line.
Unsportsmanlike conduct as determined by the referee.
6. Referees & Officials
The event shall be conducted under the supervision of appointed referees and officials.
Any dispute arising during the event shall be resolved by the referees and officials.
The decision of the referees and event officials shall be final and binding on all participants.
7. Awards
Winner: 100 Team Points
Runner-Up: 50 Team Points

FEMALE CATEGORY
1. Team Composition
Each team may nominate 2 female participants.
A total of 6 participants shall compete in this category (2 participants from each of the 3 teams).
2. Match Format
All participants shall compete individually.
The objective is to complete the designated course in the shortest possible time while remaining inside the sack at all times.
Timing shall begin when the referee signals the start.
Timing shall end when the participant crosses the finish line.
3. Tournament Format
The preliminary stage shall consist of 2 sets.
Each set shall have 3 participants, with 1 participant from each team competing simultaneously.
At the end of both sets, the 3 fastest valid participants shall qualify for the Final.
4. Final Match & Tie-Breaker Rules
The 3 qualified participants shall compete simultaneously in the Final.
The participant recording the fastest valid time in the Final shall be declared the Winner.
The participant recording the second-fastest valid time in the Final shall be declared the Runner-Up.
If two or more participants are tied for qualification to the Final, a Tie-Breaker Race shall be conducted.
If the Final results in a tie, a Tie-Breaker Race shall be conducted immediately.
5. Penalties & Disqualifications
Penalty Offences
If a participant falls, they must get up and continue from the point at which they fell.
No restart or re-run shall be granted for falls.
The sack must remain above the knees throughout the race.
If the sack falls below the knees and the referee determines that an advantage has been gained, the participant must return to their original starting position and restart the race.
The timer shall continue running during the restart.
Disqualification Offences
Intentionally pushing, shoving, or obstructing another participant.
Leaving the designated lane.
Receiving physical assistance from any person.
Running outside the sack.
Deliberately removing the sack before crossing the finish line.
Unsportsmanlike conduct as determined by the referee.
6. Referees & Officials
The event shall be conducted under the supervision of appointed referees and officials.
Any dispute arising during the event shall be resolved by the referees and officials.
The decision of the referees and event officials shall be final and binding on all participants.
7. Awards
Winner: 100 Team Points
Runner-Up: 50 Team Points

RELAY CATEGORY
1. Team Composition
Each team shall consist of 4 participants.
Teams may decide their own running order before the race begins.
Registered substitutes may be used prior to the start of the race in accordance with event substitution rules.
2. Match Format
The relay race shall be conducted over 100 metres.
Each runner shall complete approximately 25 metres before handing the sack to the next participant.
The sack must be transferred within the designated exchange zone.
3. Tournament Format
All three teams shall compete simultaneously in a single relay race.
The team completing the relay in the shortest valid time shall be declared the Winner.
The team with the second-shortest valid time shall be declared the Runner-Up.
4. Final Match & Tie-Breaker Rules
No separate Final shall be conducted.
In the event of a tie, a Tie-Breaker Relay Race shall be conducted.
5. Penalties & Disqualifications
Penalty Offences
If a participant falls, they may get up and continue from the point at which they fell.
If the sack is dropped during a handoff, it must be recovered and transferred correctly before continuing.
The sack must remain above the knees throughout each leg of the relay.
If the sack falls below the knees and the referee determines that an advantage has been gained, the participant must return to the start of their relay leg and restart that leg.
The timer shall continue running during the restart.
Disqualification Offences
Improper exchange outside the designated handoff zone.
Intentionally obstructing another team.
Leaving the designated lane.
Receiving physical assistance from any person.
Running outside the sack.
Unsportsmanlike conduct as determined by the referee.
6. Referees & Officials
The event shall be conducted under the supervision of appointed referees and officials.
Any dispute arising during the event shall be resolved by the referees and officials.
The decision of the referees and event officials shall be final and binding on all participants.
7. Awards
Winner: 100 Team Points
Runner-Up: 50 Team Points

Gifts and Certificates will be distributed on the same day.
